name: Routing Protocol Operation
description: Routing protocols build forwarding tables by sharing reachability information: distance-vector protocols exchange distance tables with neighbors while link-state protocols flood link advertisements to build a full topology, each then computes shortest paths and recomputes routes whenever the topology changes, converging on a consistent routing table.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Router knows neighbors"]
    N2["Exchange routing info"]
    N3{"DV or link-state?"}
    N4["DV: share distance table"]
    N5["LS: flood link states"]
    N6["Build topology / table"]
    N7["Run shortest-path"]
    N8{"Topology changed?"}
    N9["Recompute routes"]
    N10["Converged routing table"]
    N11["Forward by routing table"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|distance-vector| N4
    N3 -->|link-state| N5
    N4 --> N6
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|yes| N9
    N9 -->|update| N6
    N8 -->|no| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Computer Networks (5th ed.)
sources.authors: Tanenbaum, A. S.; Wetherall, D. J.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
